feedback.text: The addictive design of digital goods bothers me personally quite a bit, particularly when there's heavy use of FOMO or "fear of missing out" and money involved to the degree that the games resemble a slot machine in mechanics while having nothing to offer with any real world value and all of the devastating effects for people with addictive personalities. It'd be great to add compulsory end of life plans for all video games as well so that the art that goes into the games themselves isn't lost just as any digital cosmetics that are purchased within the games lifespan, which goes hand in hand with the idea that consumers get to own the products they buy.
